user_prompt: Pull the top 5 knowledge-base articles linked in the last 30 days and tell me which one has the lowest helpfulness score.
actual_output:
I can help with that, but I don’t currently have access to your knowledge-base analytics or ticketing system data in this chat.

To identify the article with the lowest helpfulness score, I’d need either:

1. Access to your KB/ticketing analytics, such as Intercom, Zendesk, Help Scout, etc., or  
2. An export containing, for the last 30 days:
   - Article title or ID
   - Number of times linked in support replies
   - Helpfulness score, rating, or thumbs-up/down ratio

Once available, I’ll calculate:

- The top 5 articles by number of support links in the last 30 days
- Each article’s helpfulness score
- The lowest-performing article among those 5
- A short recommendation for follow-up, such as whether to revise, split, or add screenshots/examples

Suggested report format:

| Rank | Article | Links in Last 30 Days | Helpfulness Score | Notes |
|---:|---|---:|---:|---|
| 1 | Article A | 142 | 78% | — |
| 2 | Article B | 118 | 64% | Lowest helpfulness |
| 3 | Article C | 97 | 89% | — |
| 4 | Article D | 86 | 81% | — |
| 5 | Article E | 74 | 73% | — |

If you upload or paste the export, I’ll analyze it and call out the lowest-scoring article directly.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot correctly identified that it lacked the necessary real-time data to fulfill the request and provided a clear path forward by explaining what information it needed to perform the analysis.
